title: requests — authorization policies
description: One policy class — `AccessRequestPolicy` — with abilities on both `sanctum` (tenant users submitting + reviewing) and `platform_admin` (Academorix ops for cross-tenant compliance). Guard-aware — each ability checks the caller's guard first and refuses cross-guard actions.

abilities.name: create
requires: user is authenticated on sanctum guard
user is a TenantMember of the tenant containing the target resource
abilities.description: Any authenticated TenantMember can submit a request. There is NO permission-string gate on submission — the whole POINT of access requests is to be self-service for users who lack permissions. Refused only if the caller is not a tenant member (403) or the target resource is not in their tenant (RESOURCE_NOT_IN_CALLER_TENANT 403).

abilities.name: view
requires: access-requests.view AND (caller is requester OR caller is approver OR caller is resource owner)
abilities.description: Show a specific access request. Visibility is restricted so approvers can't browse arbitrary users' requests. The caller is a legitimate observer when they participate in one of three roles for this specific request: requester, resolved approver, resource owner (i.e. created_by on the target).

abilities.name: viewAny
abilities.description: List invitations in the caller's tenant. Default filter still applies (caller sees their own + those they approve; admins with `access-requests.manage_any` see all). Enforcement: admins get a wider scope; regular users see only rows where they participate.

abilities.name: withdraw
requires: access-requests.withdraw AND request.requester_id === caller.id AND request.status === 'pending'
abilities.description: Requester withdraws their own pending request. Only the original requester can withdraw — no admin override in Wave 1b (adds complexity for a corner case).

abilities.name: approve
requires: access-requests.approve AND caller.id in requirement.resolved_approvers[] AND requirement.status === 'pending'
abilities.description: Approver decision — approve. Delegation-aware: if the caller is a delegate of a named approver via `access/delegation`, the check passes.

abilities.name: reject
requires: access-requests.reject AND caller.id in requirement.resolved_approvers[] AND requirement.status === 'pending'
reject comment MUST be present (min 10 chars) — enforced at the DTO validation layer, not here

abilities.name: viewAnyPlatform
abilities.description: Cross-tenant list for Academorix ops. Filters + audit context capture on every list read (SOC 2 CC6.1).

notes: Approver membership check goes through `AccessRequestApproverResolver` which consults `role_delegations` — so a delegate of a named approver passes the `approve`/`reject` policy without needing to be in the raw resolved_approvers[] list.

The `view` ability is DELIBERATELY more permissive than requester-only — approvers need to see the request BEFORE they've decided (or after), resource owners have a legitimate audit interest, and the tenant admin (via `access-requests.manage_any` scoped to sanctum) sees everything. Rows outside these participants are hidden by default.

No `updateRequest` ability — access requests are immutable once submitted. The only mutation is withdraw (by requester) or approve/reject (by approver). Every other 'update' is a new request entirely.
